consistent with output from the concurrent `duplicates` check.

*[Bug 17303] geterrmessage(), but ignoring LANG environment variable*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=17303>

Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |ikrylov@disroot.org

--- Comment #3 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
R-4.2.0 introduced `Sys.setLanguage()`, fixing Bug 18055 and making it possible
to call `Sys.setLanguage("en")`, then retry the failing operation to see the
error message in English. While it's not exactly `getenglisherrmessage()` and
won't help with error messages that are not reproducible, could it be
considered "good enough", closing this feature request?

text:
*[Bug 8117] whislist*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=8117>

Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |ikrylov@disroot.org

--- Comment #2 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
Since `r45513` (2008-04-27, released in 2.8.0) there's support for `??`, which
makes retrying a failed `?` query as a search as easy as pressing "Up arrow",
"Home", "?", and "Enter".

There doesn't seem to have been enough interest during the past >20 years in
adding an argument `try.search = getOption("help.try.search", FALSE)` to the
`help()` function (and possibly the `?` operator) that would make it
short-circuit to `help.search()` automatically.

I think this feature request can be closed.

text:
*[Bug 19023] New: as() fails to pick up ANY method via S3 inheritance*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19023>

            Bug ID: 19023
           Summary: as() fails to pick up ANY method via S3 inheritance
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: S4methods
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>

Converting from r-devel thread:
<https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-devel/2026-March/084409.html>

Minimal example:

```
setClass("integer64")
setAs("ANY", "integer64", \(from) from)
x <- structure(1e10, class=c("foo", "integer64"))
as(x, "integer64")
# Error in as(x, "integer64") :
#   internal problem in as(): “foo” is(object, "integer64") is TRUE, but the
metadata asserts that the 'is' relation is FALSE
```

As Martin pointed out, it's not great that the user can see such "internal
problem".

For more context, this was originally seen in the {bit64} package. We had
rewritten our `c()` S3 method using `as()` for generic coercion, which we had
to refactor to use the S3 `as.*` method directly instead.

 - <https://github.com/r-lib/bit64/pull/253>
 - <https://github.com/r-lib/bit64/pull/302>

`setClass("foo")` / `setClass("foo", contains="integer64")` /
`setOldClass("foo")` make the issue go away, too, but in context that would be
a breaking change to a downstream non-S4 package.

Honestly I'm a bit lost the right solution here, but it _does_ seem like we
misused `as()` by employing it in an S3 context.

text:
*[Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18928>

--- Comment #34 from Luke Tierney (<mailto:luke@stat.uiowa.edu|luke@stat.uiowa.edu>) ---
(In reply to Lionel Henry from comment #33)
> Created attachment 3635
> Scope dots accessors in direct frame

Thanks!

Would you need `R_findDotsEnv` to be in the API? If so we'll need to add it to
WRE.

With skipping over non-`DOTSEXP` values for `...` there are some small changes
in behavior for the strange cse of someone assigning to `...`:

In R 4.5.2:

```
> (function(...) { "..." <- 1; ...elt(1)})(1)
Error in (function(...) { : the ... list contains fewer than 1 element
> (function(...) local({ "..." <- 1; ...elt(1)}))(1)
Error in eval(quote({ : the ... list contains fewer than 1 element
> (function(...) local({ "..." <- 1:2; ...length()}))(1)
[1] 0
> (function(...) { "..." <- 1:2; ...length()})(1)
[1] 0
```

In R-devel r89667:
```
> (function(...) { "..." <- 1; ...elt(1)})(1)
Error in (function(...) { : bad ... value
> (function(...) local({ "..." <- 1; ...elt(1)}))(1)
Error in eval(quote({ : bad ... value
> (function(...) local({ "..." <- 1:2; ...length()}))(1)
[1] 0
> (function(...) { "..." <- 1:2; ...length()})(1)
[1] 0
```

With this patch:
```
> (function(...) { "..." <- 1; ...elt(1)})(1)
Error in (function(...) { : 
  ..1 used in an incorrect context, no ... to look in
> (function(...) local({ "..." <- 1; ...elt(1)}))(1)
[1] 1
> (function(...) local({ "..." <- 1:2; ...length()}))(1)
[1] 1
> (function(...) { "..." <- 1:2; ...length()})(1)
Error in (function(...) { : 
  incorrect context: the current call has no '...' to look in
```

I think I prefer the behavior after the patch.

We probably will want to expose the `.Internal`s at the R level eventually,
so probably better to write them so the R wrappers can be

```
dotForcedExpr <- function(i, env = parent.frame(), inherits = TRUE)
    .Internal(dotForcedExpression(i, env, inherits))
```

text:
*[Bug 19022] Please add a check.names= argument to rbind.data.frame().*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19022>

Elin Waring (<mailto:elin.waring@gmail.com|elin.waring@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |elin.waring@gmail.com

--- Comment #1 from Elin Waring (<mailto:elin.waring@gmail.com|elin.waring@gmail.com>) ---
This makes sense to me because 

> The cbind data frame method is just a wrapper for data.frame(..., check.names = FALSE)

In `rbind()` when `data.frame()` is called the check.names options is not
specified either way. 

That said, specifically this sequence

```
l1 <- data.frame(`a/b` = 1, c = 2, check.names = FALSE)
l2 <- data.frame(`a/b` = 3, c = 4, check.names = FALSE)

# Column name NOT converted:
rbind(l1, l2) 
```
>  a/b c
>1   1 2
>2   3 4

and even though he documentation and behavior is a bit confusing when it comes
to lists, I don't think it is necessarily expected that generic.data.frame 
will work for objects that are not data frames.
Along those lines, if you just use rbind() the names are preserved but the
resulting object is not a data frame or a list. 

```
> l1 <- list(`a/b` = 1, c = 2)
> l2 <- list(`a/b` = 3, c = 4)
> 
> # Column name converted:
> rbind(l1, l2) 
   a/b c
l1 1   2
l2 3   4
> rbind(l1, l2) |> class()
[1] "matrix" "array" 

```

text:
*[Bug 19019] interaction() should warn when coalescing levels*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19019>

--- Comment #6 from Elin Waring (<mailto:elin.waring@gmail.com|elin.waring@gmail.com>) ---
I've been trying to find an example of the need to merge (that is, getting two
identical strings) which does not involve the separator. 

Meanwhile, looking at the history, it seems that there was poor performance
when there were large numbers of levels.  Some of this was not that old (2025).

Related issues

bug 18223
bug 18276

But also back in 2010 there was an issue with duplications. 

interaction() produced malformed factors when levels were duplicated,
segfaulting split().
<https://github.com/r-devel/r-svn/commit/fffd0df7b5893f1e6c24c2bee94adc4fa3479775>

faster interaction(drop=T)

<https://github.com/r-devel/r-svn/commit/f57f27cc655416a9cef28a81bb0cd932b58e808e>

interaction() now checks its levels are unique

<https://github.com/r-devel/r-svn/commit/9fd69f9b7e336685760ad416aa45cac12a5453d3>


I guess what happened is that they tried to check for unique but didn't reckon
with the sep  issue.  As a practical matter, I think that changing the sep to
something unlikely to be in a variable name would avoid most clashes.  But
also, maybe there should also be a check for whether sep is the same as the
ending of any variable names.

elements.text: [Bug 19019] interaction() should warn when coalescing levels

) ---
I've been trying to find an example of the need to merge (that is, getting two
identical strings) which does not involve the separator. 

Meanwhile, looking at the history, it seems that there was poor performance
when there were large numbers of levels.  Some of this was not that old (2025).

Related issues

bug 18223
bug 18276

But also back in 2010 there was an issue with duplications. 

interaction() produced malformed factors when levels were duplicated,
segfaulting split().
interaction() now checks its levels are unique

I guess what happened is that they tried to check for unique but didn't reckon
with the sep  issue.  As a practical matter, I think that changing the sep to
something unlikely to be in a variable name would avoid most clashes.  But
also, maybe there should also be a check for whether sep is the same as the
ending of any variable names.
